summary: Round 12 is complete and the tip card went 5 from 5: Dolphins, Bulldogs, Warriors, Sea Eagles and Cowboys all won. No charity bet was locked, so the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains unchanged at 2-2 and -$3.50.

scoreboard.note: The Dolphins held on despite missing key Queensland Origin names and defending long second-half pressure.
The clean roster-depletion read landed, with Canterbury punishing a Storm side missing major Origin talent.
The Warriors stayed too clean for the winless Dragons and confirmed the strongest Saturday read.
The trap-game warning was real: Manly survived a late Titans scare and only won by two.
North Queensland closed the round cleanly at home while South Sydney's missing stars kept their ceiling lower.

liveAlerts.text: Round 12 is complete: TipBot went 5 from 5 on the shortened Origin-week slate.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger stays at 2-2 and -$3.50 because no Round 12 charity bet was locked.
Bulldogs versus Storm was the cleanest roster-tilt matchup and it landed 30-20.
Sea Eagles versus Titans was correctly flagged as dangerous, finishing only 12-10.
Warriors over Dragons and Cowboys over Rabbitohs both validated the stronger-side Sunday and Saturday reads.
Validated read: do not change the 5 from 5 result without re-checking official NRL scores.
